summary: Officially documented TDR DDI timeout reset. This app write restores TdrDdiDelay to Microsoft's documented default of 5 seconds, but Microsoft warns that TDR keys are for driver testing and debugging rather than normal end-user tuning.

setting.name: Graphics TDR DDI Delay
setting.casual_explanation: Windows gives graphics-driver threads a limited amount of time to leave the driver during TDR recovery. This setting controls that delay.
setting.why_people_change_it: People usually change it only when undoing manual TDR experiments or driver-debugging edits.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Restoring the documented default is useful when cleaning up graphics-driver experiments, but this is still a driver-debugging control rather than a normal consumer tweak.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents TdrDdiDelay under HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\GraphicsDrivers and states that the default value is 5 seconds. The same Microsoft page warns that TDR keys are intended for testing or debugging during driver development, not as routine end-user tuning.

allowed_values.label: Microsoft default DDI delay
allowed_values.meaning: The TDR DDI timeout is set to Microsoft's documented default of 5 seconds.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes TdrDdiDelay = 5 on the documented GraphicsDrivers path.

windows_defaults.label: Microsoft documented default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Systems restoring the documented TDR DDI delay baseline

states.rationale: Microsoft documents 5 seconds as the default TdrDdiDelay value.

windows_defaults.notes: This record is intended for resetting to the documented baseline rather than publishing a casual tweak.

recommended_profiles.label: Reset to Microsoft default
intended_for: Systems returning to the documented baseline

states.rationale: Restore the documented default DDI timeout.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You are removing custom TDR edits and want the Microsoft baseline back.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You are treating TDR timing keys as routine optimization knobs.
tradeoffs: The documented Microsoft default is restored.
The setting remains a driver-debugging surface, not a general-user recommendation.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Testing and debugging TDR during driver development
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents TdrDdiDelay under HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\GraphicsDrivers and states that the default value is 5 seconds, while warning that TDR keys are for targeted driver testing and debugging only.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes TdrDdiDelay = 5 for the current tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the exact path and default value, and the app writes that documented default as a reset-to-baseline action with a clean rollback story.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: KeyPath : HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\GraphicsDrivers; KeyValue : TdrDdiDelay; ValueType : REG_DWORD; ValueData : Number of seconds to leave the driver. The default value is 5 seconds.
validation_proof.notes: Microsoft's TDR registry keys page documents the exact GraphicsDrivers runtime path, the TdrDdiDelay value name, and the documented default of 5 seconds.
